question.text: A commodity's spot price is Rs 4,000. Finance cost is 10% p.a. and the convenience yield is 6% p.a. What is the fair value of a 6-month futures contract (simple interest, no storage cost)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** F = S + C - Y. Finance cost = 4,000 × 0.10 × (6/12) = Rs 200. Convenience yield = 4,000 × 0.06 × (6/12) = Rs 120. F = 4,000 + 200 - 120 = Rs 4,080. **The Trap:** Rs 4,200 ignores convenience yield. Rs 4,320 adds convenience yield. Rs 3,920 subtracts both. Convenience yield always reduces the futures premium. **Cross-Exam Context:** Convenience yield calculations are unique to commodity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A 6% convenience yield on a 10% financing cost means the effective carry is only 4% — industrial users valuing physical possession significantly compress the futures premium.
